The U.S. is proposing a sharp increase in citizenship application fees, a move that could disproportionately impact Indian immigrants.

The proposal would raise the naturalization fee by about 75% and eliminate fee waivers for many applicants, significantly increasing the cost of becoming a U.S. citizen.

With Indians among the largest groups of green card holders pursuing U.S. citizenship, the higher fees could create an additional financial hurdle for thousands of eligible applicants.

reports.text:
Trent Star store expansion to accelerate in Fy27 , LFL  on recovery path
Maintain Buy with Target of Rs 3500.

Trent's near-term pain is real but temporary — the long-term architecture is intact and getting bigger.

Through this note - we just want to convey don't confuse a speed bump (LFL dip, Star restructuring) with a roadblock. The store count runway is massive (Westside 300 to 700 stores, Zudio 960 to 5000, Star Bazaar still present only in 11.citiea huge runway for growth plus international market, Burnt Toast , Samoh are optionality which not built in future nos), the formats are proven, and the management is executing ahead of its own targets. With recovery now visible, this is a good time to stay invested.

We build in a CAGR of 21%/19%/17% in consolidated revenue/pre-IND AS EBITDA/adj. PAT over FY26-28E.

JPM on IT Services

India IT faces an uncertain demand environment from an unprecedented confluence of technology & business cycle headwinds from GenAI-led deflation & geopolitics.

Enterprises face FUD (fear, uncertainty, doubt) from changing tech & geopolitics, with tech services budgets crowded out from spending on AI tokens and cloud keeping industry growth recovery prospects uncertain. 

Indeed, IT services industry has been stuck at 2-3% revenue growth over last three years.
With AI deflation still only in Year 2, see further headwinds to growth over next two years

Do not expect large-caps to hit mid-single-digit growth & hover around 3-4% revenue growth.

Reverse DCF sanity checks drive target P/E multiple cuts of 10-25% across the board

Downgrade HCLT to UW from N, TP cut to Rs 1000 from Rs 1370

Downgrade Wipro to UW from N, TP cut to Rs 160 from Rs 200

Downgrade TATA TECH to UW – TP Rs 540 (believe its sharp price action more than bakes in outperformance)

TRUMP REJECTS INDIA TARIFF DATA, CALLS U.S. FIGURES ‘BULLSHIT’

President Donald Trump reportedly dismissed official U.S. government data on Indian tariffs, saying, “These are bullshit numbers,” during a discussion on trade barriers.

Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick pushed back, responding that the figures were in fact official U.S. government statistics.

The exchange highlights Trump’s longstanding focus on India’s tariff regime and underscores ongoing tensions over market access and trade reciprocity between the United States and India.

reports.text:
TRUMP REJECTS INDIA TARIFF DATA, CALLS U.S. FIGURES ‘BULLSHIT’

President Donald Trump reportedly dismissed official U.S. government data on Indian tariffs, saying, “These are bullshit numbers,” during a discussion on trade barriers.

Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick pushed back, responding that the figures were in fact official U.S. government statistics.

The exchange highlights Trump’s longstanding focus on India’s tariff regime and underscores ongoing tensions over market access and trade reciprocity between the United States and India.

Accenture raises buyback target to $7.5 billion; stock jumps in pre-market trade

Accenture shares climbed about 2% in pre-market trading on Tuesday after the global IT services company increased its fiscal 2026 share repurchase target by $2 billion, taking the total planned buyback to $7.5 billion.

The enhanced repurchase programme represents a 62% year-on-year increase and will be executed by Aug. 31, 2026, under the share repurchase authority approved by the company's Board of Directors in September 2025.

The additional $2 billion comes on top of the $300 million Accenture had already earmarked for repurchases during the current quarter, lifting its expected fourth-quarter buybacks to $2.3 billion.

reports.text:
🗞 BEAT THE STREET BULLETIN 🗞
Date: 23 June 2026
Telegram: https://t.me/BeatTheStreetnews

📍 ORDER WIN

RAILTEL: Bags ₹334.52 Cr order from Ministry of Railways for e-Office upgradation across zonal railways and administrative units, to be executed till 2031.

INTERARCH BUILDING SOLUTIONS: Secures ~₹165 Cr order for design, engineering, manufacturing, supply and erection of pre-engineered steel building system over 15 months.

MARINE ELECTRICALS: Wins ₹75.19 Cr orders from STT Global Data Centres and Princeton Digital Group for power distribution systems.

KRISHNA DEFENCE: Receives ₹45.64 Cr Ministry of Defence order for special steel products to be executed in 8 months.

KRISHNA INSTITUTE OF MEDICAL SCIENCES: Gets listing approval for 1.98 crore QIP shares issued to institutional buyers.

WE WIN: Secures ₹8.70 Cr MPRDC order for accident response system and traffic management centre with 3-year contract.

SHREE MARUTINANDAN TUBES: Bags ₹2.24 Cr order from Value Fashion Retail (Arvind Group) for piping works.

DYNAMIC SERVICES & SECURITY: Subsidiary wins ₹5.47 lakh Indian Railways battery supply order for diesel locomotive applications.

SHAYONA ENGINEERING: Receives ₹25.56 lakh industrial piping order from domestic customer for 15-day execution.

---

📍 ACQUISITION

MAXIMUS INTERNATIONAL: Acquires 40% stake in Quebec Petroleum for ₹18.43 Cr, marking entry into domestic lubricant manufacturing.

BATLIBOI: Approves 100% acquisition of Penta Automation Systems for ~₹19.8 Cr, strengthening robotics and industrial automation presence.

NEPHROCARE HEALTH SERVICES: Philippines arm acquires dialysis centre assets for ~₹1.2 Cr, expanding overseas healthcare network.

---

📍 COLLABORATION

QUESS CORP: Partners with IGS and IPA to build Indo-Japan GCC corridor to support Japanese firms setting up capability centres in India.

NLC INDIA & IOC: Sign MoU to form JV for renewable energy projects including solar, wind, BESS, pumped hydro and green hydrogen.

HCLTECH: Joins TM Forum Catalyst Program to develop AI-led telecom automation framework with global operators.

STRIDERS IMPEX: UAE subsidiary signs license agreement with Hasbro for merchandising rights of major global toy brands till 2029.

---

📍 LEADERSHIP CHANGES

IRCTC: CMD Sanjay Kumar Jain resigns effective July 20, 2026; Railways Ministry to assign additional charge temporarily.

IRCON INTERNATIONAL: Saleem Ahmad assigned additional charge as CMD from July 1, 2026 following retirement of current CMD.

UNITED INTERACTIVE: Company Secretary & Compliance Officer Nilesh Amrutkar resigns, effective July 1, 2026.

FRUITION VENTURE: Independent Director Shefali Kesarwani resigns citing personal commitments.

METRO BRANDS: Appoints Manoj Juneja as Chief Business Officer – Sports Division.

---

📍 FUNDRAISING

GEECEE VENTURES: Increases stake in GMR Power & Urban Infra by investing ₹1.05 Cr via open market purchase of 1 lakh shares.

KRISHNA INSTITUTE OF MEDICAL SCIENCES: QIP shares worth 1.98 crore equity shares get listing approval post institutional placement.

---

📍 FII STAKE CHANGES / BOUGHT-SOLD

YASH HIGHVOLTAGE: Investors sell 2.27% stake via open market transactions between Aug 2025–June 2026, reducing holding to 3.46%.

---

📍 CREDIT RATINGS

TD POWER SYSTEMS: CRISIL upgrades long-term rating to AA-/Stable and short-term to A1+ for ₹488 Cr facilities.

KEI INDUSTRIES: ICRA reaffirms AA+ (Stable) and A1+ ratings for ₹3,810 Cr facilities.

MAHAMAYA STEEL INDUSTRIES: Brickwork assigns BBB+ Stable and A2 ratings for fund-based facilities.

---

📍 CLARIFICATION

GEECEE VENTURES / GMR POWER: Disclosure clarifies ₹1.05 Cr investment increasing holding to 0.03% stake under SEBI norms.

📍 NEW PROJECTS

BELDING INDIA: Forms JV for Battery Energy Storage Systems and renewable energy projects with HD Fabcon.

📍 LAUNCHES

---

📍 IT NEWS

WIPRO: Expands partnership with Palo Alto Networks for AI-powered cybersecurity MDR services.

ORACLE: Cuts ~21,000 jobs in FY26 as it restructures while increasing AI investments and capex.

📍 Q2 / BUSINESS UPDATE

---

📍 USFDA / REGULATORY

📍 OTHERS

PERSISTENT SYSTEMS: AI-driven growth outlook remains intact with BFSI and healthcare strength.

INFO EDGE: Says deeptech will take 7–10 years to play out meaningfully in portfolio.

EMKAY GLOBAL: Flags AI-led market volatility, capital rotation and mixed global sentiment.

US–IRAN: Develops communication channels; $12B frozen assets release and temporary oil sanction easing.

S&P GLOBAL ENERGY: Highlights structural reset in upstream oil markets and volatility in supply outlook.

• Customer: EPC Company, New Delhi
• Product: 20MVA, 66/11KV Power Transformers

• Execution Timeline: Approximately 11 Months

LORD'S MARK INDUSTRIES : COMMISSIONS NEW IVD FACILITY AHEAD OF SCHEDULE 🏥🧪

• 🏭 Company has commissioned a new 14,000 sq. ft. In Vitro Diagnostics (IVD) manufacturing facility at Vasai, Maharashtra

• ⏩ Facility became operational 2 months ahead of schedule, against the planned August 2026 launch

• 📈 Total operational manufacturing footprint has increased from 20,000 sq. ft. to 34,000 sq. ft.

• 🧬 New unit will manufacture a portfolio of 56 ELISA test kits along with a new range of rapid diagnostic test kits

• 🔬 R&D division has been dedicated to the memory of Late Shri Ratan Tata and will focus on indigenous molecular biology and genetic science research

• 🏗️ Company also plans a third facility at Vasai, focused on early cancer detection research, expected to commence operations from January 2029

• 🤖 Long-term vision includes establishing a large-scale medical device manufacturing ecosystem focused on AI-enabled and imaging-based diagnostic devices

🟢 Impact: Positive (Capacity expansion ahead of schedule strengthens diagnostics manufacturing capabilities, supports future growth and enhances the company's healthcare presence.)

TVS SRICHAKRA (EUROGRIP TYRES) : OPENS 5 EXCLUSIVE STORES IN HYDERABAD 🏍️🛞

• 🏪 Eurogrip Tyres has opened 5 new exclusive retail stores in Hyderabad, Telangana

• 📈 Expansion is aimed at strengthening the brand's retail footprint and customer reach in a key two-wheeler market

• 🛵 Stores will offer Eurogrip's complete range of tyres for scooters, commuter bikes, adventure motorcycles and superbikes

• 🔧 Outlets will also provide tyre fitment, tyre care, puncture repair, air pressure checks and warranty-related services

• 🌍 Hyderabad expansion is part of the company's broader strategy to strengthen its branded retail network across India

• 📍 Eurogrip already operates retail experience stores in Chennai, Mysuru, Patna, Farrukhabad, Ahmedabad, Rajkot, Panipat, Bahraich, Ludhiana, Delhi and Alappuzha

• 🤝 Initiative focuses on enhancing customer experience and improving brand visibility in high-growth markets

🟢 Impact: Positive (Retail network expansion strengthens market presence, improves customer access and supports long-term volume growth for the tyre business.)

reports.text:
WIPRO : EXPANDS PARTNERSHIP WITH PALO ALTO NETWORKS FOR AI-POWERED CYBER DEFENSE 🔐🤖

• 🤝 Wipro has expanded its partnership with Palo Alto Networks to offer AI-driven Managed Detection and Response (MDR) services

• 🛡️ The solution combines Palo Alto Cortex XSIAM with CyberShield and Wipro's managed security capabilities

• ⚡ Platform leverages AI, machine learning and automation to improve threat detection, prediction and response

• 📊 Aims to reduce alert noise, improve analyst productivity and enhance focus on critical cyber threats

• 🌐 Services will be delivered through Wipro's eight Cyber Defense Centers (CDCs) globally

• 🚀 Offering is powered by Wipro Intelligence, WEGA and WINGS AI platforms

• 🏢 Partnership builds on existing collaboration across cloud, network and security transformation projects

• 🎯 Targets growing enterprise demand for AI-led cybersecurity operations and security automation

🟢 Impact: Positive (Strengthens Wipro's cybersecurity portfolio, enhances AI capabilities and creates opportunities to win higher-value managed security contracts.)

• 💰 Consideration amount will be finalized at the time of completion of the Securities Purchase Agreement

reports.text:
We have released today  our Textile Thematic Note

Indian textiles are at a rare multi-year inflection point driven by four simultaneous catalysts. 

First, US tariffs on India have normalised to ~10%, putting India on par with Bangladesh and Vietnam for the first time. 

Second, the India-UK FTA is concluded and India-EU FTA is near — eliminating a structural 8–12% tariff handicap that held back Indian exporters for years.
 
Third, US retailer inventories are clean after 2–3 years of correction — fresh orders are flowing. 

Fourth, China's share in global apparel trade has halved from 37% to 15% in the US, creating a large and growing white space for India. 

Listed Indian textile companies are direct beneficiaries — they are _large, compliant, globally certified, and now expanding capacity aggressively.

reports.text:
Sarvam AI

Major Milestone
- Became India's first AI unicorn
- Demonstrates India's capability to build foundation AI companies

Funding Challenge
- Sarvam AI recently raised ~$234 Mn
- China's DeepSeek reportedly secured ~$7.4 Bn
- Highlights the large capital gap in global AI competition

India vs Global AI Funding
- India attracted ~$11.1 Bn private AI investment (2013-2024)
- US invested ~$285.9 Bn in AI in 2025 alone
- Capital availability remains India's biggest scaling challenge

Why Foundation AI Is Different
- Requires massive spending on:
  - GPUs and compute infrastructure
  - Proprietary datasets
  - AI research talent
  - Model training and deployment
- Funding needs can run into billions of dollars

Key Ecosystem Gap
- India has strong AI talent and entrepreneurs
- Early-stage funding ecosystem is improving
- Missing the large-scale, patient capital needed for global AI leaders

Industry View
- Foundation AI should be treated as strategic infrastructure
- Commercialization cycles are much longer than traditional software
- Companies may require multiple funding rounds before meaningful revenues emerge

What India Needs
- Larger deeptech-focused capital pools
- Long-term investors willing to support multi-year development cycles
- Stronger scale-up funding ecosystem between startup and global leadership stage

Key Takeaway
- Sarvam AI's success proves India can build ambitious foundation-model companies.
- The next challenge is not innovation, but securing the patient, multi-billion-dollar capital required to scale globally.
- Without a stronger scale-up funding ecosystem, India risks creating AI innovators that struggle to compete with heavily funded global rivals.

India Core Sector Growth (May 2026)

Headline
- Core sector growth slowed to 0.5%
- Lowest level in seven months
- 5 of 8 core sectors contracted

Major Declines
- Refinery Products: -8.7%
- Coal: -9.3%
- Natural Gas: -4.9%
- Crude Oil: -4.6%
- Fertilisers: -0.9%

Key Growth Areas
- Electricity: +8.7%
- Cement: +8.4%
- Steel: +5.0%

Reasons for Weakness
- Lower refinery output
- Higher crude imports
- Soft global energy prices
- Natural gas supply issues
- Weak petro-product exports

Positive Pockets
- Strong power demand due to higher temperatures
- Healthy construction activity supporting cement and steel demand

IIP Impact
- Core industries account for 40.27% of IIP weight
- IIP growth expected to slow to 2-3% in May from 4.9% in April

Key Takeaway
- Energy-related sectors were the biggest drag on growth.
- Cement, steel and electricity remained resilient, indicating infrastructure and construction demand is still healthy.
- Weak core sector growth may lead to softer industrial production numbers in the near term.

India-US Trade Deal

Government Stance
- India not negotiating under deadline pressure
- Deadline pressure lies on the US, not India
- US additional tariffs expire on July 24

Key Development
- US Trade Representative Jamieson Greer to visit India on July 23-24
- Multiple rounds of trade talks scheduled

India's Priority
- Preferential market access for Indian exporters
- Better competitiveness versus ASEAN and neighboring countries
- Focus on trade, technology, education and services

Interim Framework
- US agreed to reduce additional duties to 18% from 50%
- India to provide market access for US manufactured products
- India to purchase $500 billion of US goods over five years

Key Takeaway
- India is negotiating from a position of patience rather than urgency
- July 24 tariff expiry increases pressure on the US side
- Successful completion of the first phase of the trade deal would be positive for exporters and bilateral trade

reports.text:
SEBI proposes a major market structure reform that could eventually give retail investors the same Direct Market Access (DMA) facility currently available mainly to institutional investors: MC 

Key proposals:

• DMA access for all investors – Retail investors may be allowed to place orders directly into exchange trading systems through a broker’s infrastructure without dealer intervention, subject to risk controls and safeguards.  

• Commodity DMA expansion – DMA in commodity derivatives would be extended to all eligible participants, broadening access beyond current restrictions.  

• Technology rule overhaul – SEBI plans to consolidate multiple technology, cybersecurity and operational circulars governing exchanges, clearing corporations and depositories into a simplified framework, while removing outdated provisions

**Investec on Vishal Mega Mart**

**Rating & Target Price**
- Buy
- Target Price: ₹144/share

**Investment Thesis**
- India's mass retail formalisation offers one of the largest consumption opportunities
- Vishal Mega Mart is viewed as structurally well-positioned to capitalize on this trend
- Scale, sourcing capabilities and pricing strategy provide competitive advantages

**Market Position**
- Store Network: 795 stores
- Strong nationwide presence
- Significant scale advantage in value retailing

**Private Label Strength**
- Private-label contribution exceeds 74%
- Among the highest in the retail sector
- Supports superior margins and pricing flexibility
- Enhances customer value proposition

**Competitive Advantages**
- Sharp sourcing engine
- Strong pricing discipline
- Efficient merchandise management
- Ability to deliver affordable products at scale

**Growth Outlook**

**Same Store Sales Growth (SSSG)**
- Expected at 10%

**Store Expansion**
- Store additions expected at 13%

**Financial Growth**
- Revenue CAGR (FY26-29E): 18%
- EPS CAGR (FY26-29E): 21%

**Growth Drivers**
- Strong same-store growth
- Aggressive store expansion
- Private-label penetration
- Operational efficiencies
- Mass retail formalisation

**Operating Model**
- Tight cost control remains a key strength
- Focus on efficiency-led growth
- Scalable retail platform

**Return Ratios**
- Post-tax ROCE (excluding goodwill): 24%
- Reflects efficient capital allocation
- Supports long-term value creation

**Valuation**
- Trading at 42x FY28E earnings

**Why Investec Finds Valuation Attractive**
- 21% EPS CAGR expected during FY26-29
- Strong 24% post-tax ROCE
- Significant scale and execution advantages

**Peer Comparison**
- Dmart and Trent trade at a combined average of 57x FY28E consensus earnings
- Vishal Mega Mart trades at a discount despite strong growth prospects

**Key Takeaway**
- Investec views Vishal Mega Mart as one of the best-positioned beneficiaries of India's mass retail formalisation.
- A combination of 10% same-store sales growth, 13% store expansion and industry-leading private-label penetration is expected to drive 18% revenue CAGR and 21% EPS CAGR over FY26-29E.
- At 42x FY28E earnings, the stock is considered attractively valued relative to its growth profile and retail peers.

**Citi on Meesho**

**Rating & Target Price**
- Buy
- Target Price: ₹210/share

**Investment Thesis**
- Meesho is a value-focused e-commerce platform
- Possesses multiple strategic differentiators versus other e-commerce players
- Positioned uniquely within India's value-commerce ecosystem

**Scale & User Base**
- Annual Transacting Users: 264 Mn+
- User Growth: +33% YoY (Mar'26)

**Seller Ecosystem**
- Annual Transacting Sellers: 950,000+
- Seller Growth: +87% YoY
- Rapid expansion of merchant network supporting platform scale

**Business Model**
- Fully marketplace-based platform
- Asset-light operating structure
- Focused on enabling low-cost commerce
- Designed to improve efficiency for both buyers and sellers

**Competitive Advantages**

**For Sellers**
- Structurally lowers the cost of doing business
- Marketplace model reduces operating burden
- Enables access to a large nationwide customer base

**For Consumers**
- Affordable product selection
- Value-focused positioning
- Broad assortment across categories
- Competitive pricing advantage

**Technology-Led Platform**
- Leverages technology across the e-commerce value chain
- Focused on improving platform efficiency
- Strengthens accessibility and scalability
- Supports long-term ecosystem growth

**Market Opportunity**
- Positioned as an "infinite and accessible store"
- Addresses a massive consumer base
- Focus on underserved value-conscious customers
- Expanding reach across India

**Valuation**
- Valued at 50x FY29E EV/Adjusted EBITDA
- Reflects confidence in long-term growth potential
- Supported by scale, user growth and marketplace economics

**Key Growth Drivers**
- Expanding user base
- Rapid seller acquisition
- Technology-led efficiency improvements
- Marketplace-led cost advantages
- Penetration into value-commerce segments

**Key Takeaway**
- Citi views Meesho as a uniquely positioned value-commerce platform with strong user and seller growth.
- Its fully marketplace-based model lowers costs for sellers while providing affordable product selection to consumers.
- With 264 Mn+ annual transacting users and 950k+ active sellers, Meesho is seen as a scalable platform leveraging technology to build a large and accessible e-commerce ecosystem.

**Rating & Target Price**

**Key Monitorables**
- Q1FY27 jewellery growth performance
- Studded jewellery growth sustainability
- Consumer demand trends
- Impact of duty-related pricing changes

- Combined with resilient consumer demand and support from the watches business, growth momentum remains firmly intact.

**Jefferies on Insurance Sector**

**Regulatory Update**
- IRDAI has released five exposure drafts
- Covers investments, intermediaries, TPAs, reinsurers and penalties
- Signals ongoing regulatory framework refinement

**Key Readthroughs for Insurers**

**Investment Flexibility**
- Life insurers may be allowed to undertake Repo/Government Lending transactions
- Permitted up to 10% of available G-Sec holdings
- Could improve investment portfolio efficiency
- Enhances liquidity management flexibility

**Intermediary Disclosure Norms**
- Corporate agents earning commissions above ₹10 Cr annually will be required to disclose commissions every year
- Aims to improve transparency in distribution practices
- Could impact large insurance distribution platforms

**Implications for PB Fintech**
- Disclosure requirements may increase transparency around commission structures
- Relevant for large-scale insurance distribution businesses
- Investors likely to monitor implementation details

**Commission Regulations**
- Commission framework remains linked to EOM Regulations 2024
- No exposure draft released yet on this specific aspect
- Further clarity awaited from the regulator

**Sector Impact**
- Improved investment flexibility for life insurers
- Greater transparency across insurance distribution ecosystem
- Regulatory environment continues to evolve
- No immediate material earnings impact indicated

**Key Monitorables**
- Final IRDAI regulations after consultation process
- Details of EOM Regulations 2024
- Implementation timeline for commission disclosures
- Impact of repo and government lending provisions on insurer investment income

**Key Takeaway**
- The most significant proposal is allowing life insurers to deploy up to 10% of their available G-Sec holdings in repo/government lending transactions, potentially improving investment efficiency.
- Enhanced disclosure requirements for corporate agents earning over ₹10 Cr in commissions could increase transparency for insurance distributors, including platforms such as PB Fintech.

**Television Business**
- Linear TV viewership market share at a 48-week high
- Strong performance across broadcast platforms
- Improving audience share supports advertising outlook

**Growth Outlook**
- Current operating momentum creates upside risk to FY27 and FY29 growth forecasts
- Streaming and television businesses both showing strength
- FIFA rights expected to provide long-duration content advantage

**Valuation**
- Trading at approximately 1x sales
- Trading at approximately 11x FY28CL earnings
- CLSA views valuation as inexpensive relative to growth potential

**Key Positives**
- Strong Z5 user growth
- Most downloaded OTT platform
- Long-term FIFA content rights secured
- Improving TV market share
- Attractive valuation

**Key Takeaway**
- CLSA believes Zee Entertainment is benefiting from strong FIFA World Cup-driven engagement, with Z5 weekly active users doubling and becoming the most downloaded OTT platform.
- The company's long-term FIFA rights through 2034, improving television market share and inexpensive valuation at 1x sales and 11x FY28 earnings support a positive outlook.

Fujikura is raising prices on data center cables to beat outlook

Fujikura is one of the world's leading optical fiber cable (OFC) manufacturers. Like most global OFC companies, its stock delivered an extraordinary rally over the last 15 months.

However, after reporting earnings in mid-May 2026, Fujikura's shares plunged more than 40% within days.

The sharp correction was driven by concerns over production capacity bottlenecks, intensifying competition, and supply chain constraints. The company also issued annual and three-year profit guidance that came in well below consensus expectations. Investors, who were anticipating rapid capacity expansion and volume-led growth, were disappointed by the conservative outlook.

Yet, the stock has since staged an equally impressive recovery and is now approaching its previous highs.

The market eventually recognized that meaningful revenue growth did not require large incremental capital expenditure in the near term. Instead, acute global supply shortages significantly strengthened Fujikura's pricing power, enabling the company to implement price hikes of nearly 30%. The investment narrative shifted from being a volume expansion story to a pricing power and margin expansion story.

That change in perception was reinforced when Fujikura raised its profit guidance by 47% in June, validating the thesis and triggering a sharp reversal in the stock.

- FY27 could mark the start of commercialization

**Growth Engine 1: Alloys Division**

**Growth Engine 2: Relays Business**

**Opportunity**

- Relay content value: ₹250-300 per meter
- Addressable market: ~25 crore smart meters
- Large long-term domestic opportunity

**FY27 Outlook**
- Commercialization expected in H2FY27
- Customer approvals and pilot testing underway
- Potential revenue opportunity: ₹25-50 Cr
- Emerging smart infrastructure play

**Growth Engine 3: Quantum Magnetics**

**Strategic Importance**
- Focused on rare earth magnets
- Beneficiary of global supply chain diversification
- Positioned amid China's export restrictions
- Addresses strategic manufacturing opportunity

**Project Progress**
- Phase 1 implemented
- Phase 2 under development
- Block cutting, machining and surface treatment planned in FY27
- First commercial revenues expected during FY27

**Long-Term Vision**
- Target: 5,000-tonne rare earth magnet ecosystem
- Potential revenue opportunity: ₹3,000-4,000 Cr
- More than 10x current company revenue
- Multi-year execution roadmap

**Why It Matters**
- Rare earth magnets critical for:
  - Electric Vehicles
  - Defence Systems
  - Wind Turbines
  - Industrial Automation
  - Electronics Manufacturing

**Key FY27 Triggers**
- Alloys division scaling up
- Relay business commercialization
- Quantum Magnetics revenue commencement
- Transition from investment phase to execution phase

**Key Risks**
- Commercialization delays
- Customer qualification timelines
- Execution of rare earth expansion plans
- Dependence on approvals and scaling success

**Investment Thesis**
- Permanent Magnets is evolving from a niche magnet manufacturer into a diversified industrial technology company.
- The Alloys business provides near-term growth, Relays offer exposure to India's smart metering rollout, while Quantum Magnetics represents a potentially transformational long-term opportunity.

**Key Takeaway**
- For several years, the story was about investments and capacity creation.
- FY27 could become the inflection year where Alloys, Relays and Quantum Magnetics begin contributing commercially.
- The biggest long-term opportunity remains the rare earth magnet business, but successful execution of all three growth engines will determine whether the company can evolve into a much larger industrial platform over the next decade.

VIVIANA POWER TECH | FY26 INVESTOR PRESENTATION DECODE
Reg 30 filing | 15 June 2026

A Vadodara EPC contractor just told the market it wants to become
a vertically integrated Power T&D platform. The numbers behind the
ambition are real. So are the things worth watching.

THE HEADLINE NUMBERS (Standalone, FY26)
Sales: ₹501.67 Cr (up 166% YoY)
Operating Profit: ₹71.49 Cr (up 174%)
OPM: 14.25% (up 39 bps)
Net Profit: ₹50.18 Cr (up 195%)
NPM: 10.00%
EPS (diluted): ₹49.80 (up 81%)
Order book: ₹1,000+ Cr at end FY26
Consolidated revenue: around ₹533 Cr, 16x the topline since the
NSE Emerge listing in September 2022.

THE EVENT THAT MATTERS
Mainboard migration approved 21 May 2026, listed on the NSE
mainboard 2 June 2026. This opens the door to institutional flows
and improved liquidity. For a company that listed on Emerge less
than four years ago, that is a fast graduation.

THE STRATEGY: A THREE-PART FLYWHEEL
1. Power T&D EPC (the engine): EHV substations up to 400 kV,
   transmission lines 132 to 765 kV, distribution, BESS.
2. Power Equipment Manufacturing (the new bet): a greenfield
   power transformer unit near Vadodara on 14 acres, capability up
   to 400 kV, around ₹100 Cr capex, production targeted by end FY28.
   Built on the Aarsh Transformers acquisition (75% in FY24).
3. Viviana Life Spaces (the collateral engine): a real estate arm
   meant to generate around ₹100 Cr of collateral by FY30 to fund
   EPC working capital without pledging factory assets.

THE BESS ANGLE
Entered battery storage with around ₹511 Cr of secured orders.
100 MW Rajasthan (RVUNL, ₹302.46 Cr) and 65 MW Gujarat (GUVNL,
₹208.74 Cr). Management frames this as a credential ladder toward
TBCB and HVDC bidding, where single tickets run ₹1,000 to 5,000 Cr.

THE TARGET
FY30 consolidated revenue ₹2,000+ Cr, consolidated PAT ₹200+ Cr,
Debt/Equity held between 1.0 and 1.5x.

NOW THE PART MOST POSTS WILL SKIP
This is a growth story funded on a tight cash base, and honest
reading means flagging it.

Operating cash flow was just ₹0.79 Cr in FY26 against ₹50 Cr of
PAT. Almost the entire profit is sitting in working capital, not
cash. Trade receivables jumped from ₹119.83 Cr to ₹336.86 Cr.
Receivable days rose to 147. Year-end cash was ₹0.01 Cr. Growth
is being financed (financing inflow ₹52.57 Cr), with borrowings
and share capital both rising.

None of this is unusual for a DISCOM-facing EPC firm scaling fast,
and payable days improving to 108 shows real supplier trust. But
the gap between reported profit and collected cash is the single
metric to track from here. A company expanding into capex-heavy
transformer manufacturing AND real estate at the same time needs
its receivables to convert.

THE READ
Strong execution, genuine sector tailwind (₹9.2 tn India
transmission capex anticipated FY25 to FY32E), a credible
manufacturing pivot into a globally tight transformer market, and
a clean mainboard story. Tempered by a cash conversion cycle that
demands close monitoring and a real estate diversification that
not every investor will welcome.

**You don't have to see the whole staircase,
Just take the First Step.**

What FY26 really proved

TDPS spent years building quietly (OEM approvals, certifications, export relationships) and FY26 is when that infrastructure started paying out at scale. 

Revenue grew 44%, profit grew even faster, and the factory is so full that customers are questioning 3-day delays. That's not luck. That's compounding.

Management has guided ₹2,400-2,500+ crore for FY27, with FY28 pointing toward ₹3,000-3,200 crore - all on existing capacity. The large generator expansion targeting up to 200 MW hasn't even been announced yet. When it lands, it resets the ceiling.

The constraint isn't demand. It isn't capital. It's just execution - and management's language suggests they know it, and intend to deliver.

What didn't drive growth

Equally important is what didn't work - each of these tells you something useful about what FY27 and beyond might look like.

Operating leverage

Here's the part that made profit growth even better than revenue growth. TDPS runs a fixed-cost-heavy operation - plant, machinery, a skilled engineering team.

As revenue scaled to ₹590 crore per quarter, the same cost base was now spread across a much larger base. The hiring was largely done; incremental revenue was flowing through at higher margins than the reported average.

The result: PAT grew 42% while revenue grew 35%. The one active headwind is commodity inflation - copper is running near $14,000/MT and hedges are gradually running out. 

Management expects margins to hold over the next 2-3 quarters, but this is the variable to watch closely.

Going export-heavy

The second driver wasn't a segment - it was a change in who TDPS was selling to. Export orders grew at 76%, nearly 1.5× the pace of total inflow growth. By year-end, 4 out of every 5 rupees of new orders was export-linked.

Export contracts priced in USD and EUR mean that as the rupee depreciates, INR revenue rises - and since most raw materials are domestically sourced, that benefit flows largely to the bottom line. 

More importantly, export contracts tied to global OEM approvals are sticky in a way domestic orders simply aren't. Getting approved as a TDPS supplier takes years of qualification. Once you're in, the switching costs are too high to justify moving.

The engine: gas turbine generators

TDPS makes generators that sit alongside gas turbines and engines. Every turbine needs one generator. So when turbine manufacturers start producing more, TDPS's order book grows proportionally - they don't need to win new customers or fight for share. They just need their existing OEM relationships to stay intact and their customers' factories to run hot.

That's exactly what happened. Global demand for gas-based power infrastructure (driven by AI data centers needing reliable, dispatchable power) sent turbine OEMs into aggressive capacity expansion mode. Management described it simply as "massive growth, without pause."

One important nuance: all of FY26's gas turbine growth came from sub-50 MW machines. The large generator expansion (targeting up to 200 MW) that management is now planning is a completely separate, incremental story - one that hasn't contributed a single rupee yet. That's the next chapter.

Overview

FY26 was a breakout year. Consolidated revenue crossed ₹1,878 crore (+44%) and PAT grew even faster than the topline at +42%. Margins expanded.

But this wasn't broad-based, all-round growth. Three things did the work: gas turbine generators, a shift toward exports, and operating leverage. Everything else was either flat or a drag.

MORGAN STANLEY ON INDIA IT SECTOR

Accenture's Q3FY26 results and Q4 outlook missed both consensus and broker estimates

Geopolitical tensions are negatively impacting bookings and revenues, specifically within automotive and resources verticals

Managed services deal push-outs into FY27 appear driven by company-specific issues rather than broader economic shifts

Q4 growth guidance of 1-5% YoY signals an intensification of these indirect challenges

The commentary mirrors recent weak macro Betu warnings from TCS, raising the risk of widespread growth guidance cuts across Indian IT firms

Growth momentum remains under pressure with a weak Q1 start and potential spillover into Q2
